qas.a: 4.1.1. Appoints a primary and alternate fuels Responsible Officer (RO); IAW DoD 4140.25-M DoD Management of Bulk Petroleum, Natural Gas and Coal/DLA-E P-7

4.1.1.1. Enlisted members and Dep. of the AF Civilians are to complete PLMC and meet requirements in DLA-E P-7 prior to being appointed as an RO
4.1.1.2. Officers are to complete the 21-R course and meet requirements in DLA-E P-7
4.1.2. Budgets for training requirements outlines in units Designed Operational Capability (DOC) statement and those postured as rotationally available in UTC availability in DCAPES
4.1.3. Requests Official Passports for fuels personnel IAW DoD 1000.21-R and AFI 10-403; coordinates with Force Support Squadron to expedite when mission dictates

4.1.4. Provides personal protective and safety equipment required by Bioenvironmental Engineerings Industrial Hygiene Survey
4.1.5. Ensures fuels personnel arent assigned as fuels delivery escorts to areas outside FMT's span of control IAW AFI 23-204

4.1.6. Refers to Attachment 4 Personal Equipment for FARP an ABFDS Specialists, Special Purpose Clothing and Personal Equipment, AFE, Guardian Angel and Special Tactics, AFI 11-301 V1, AFE Program and AFI 11-235, FARP Operations, to ensure ABFDS and FARP specialists receive the necessary personal equipment

4.1.7. Under no circumstances will agencies outside wing level activities visit, request or obtain info about an AF fuels ops without prior coordination and approval of safeguarded and protects flights from unauthorized contact from outside agencies

4.1.8. Appoints a primary and alternate Airfield Driving Program Managers (ADPM) IAW AFI 13-213, Airfield Driving
4.1.9. At locations with cryo facilities, reinvests product production capital into plant maintenance, spare parts and training

4.2.1. Serves as world-wide Integrated Material Manager (IMM) for bulk petroleum products
4.2.2. Utilizes regional offices to monitor customer activity, capability, and operating practices for CONUS and OCONUS locations

4.2.3. Provides guidance regarding management of DWCF through DoD 4140.25-M and DLA E P-7
4.2.3.1. Assesses and approves waiver requests to requirements outlined in DoD 4140.25-M and DLA Energy Interim Policy Implementation and Procedural Guidance

4.2.4. Works with CCMD JPO to execute IMM responsibilities
4.2.5. Furnishes DD Form 448, Military Interdepartmental Purchase Request (MIPR), for environmental expenses and Sustainment, Restoration and Modernization (SRM)

4.2.6. Provides Centrally Managed Program (CMP) for capitalized facilities such as Cathodic Protection
Demolition
Dredging
Filter Seperators API
Hyrdrants
Marine Loading Arms
Piers/marine structures
Railroad Track Systems
Tanks
4.2.7 Establishes procedures for processing special fuels

4.3.1. Provides 24 hour maintenance support for fuels facilities and associated equipment
4.3.1.1. Maintains a special inventory level coordinated with FMT for fixed system filter separator elements

4.3.2. Provides detailed base grid map as requested; consolidated maps/layered and must show
Liquid fuel system layout of all piping and facilities
Base disaster preparedness layout with cordon plotter
Flightline layout

4.3.3. Provides detailed schematic charts and coordinates with FMT for operating checklists on each fuel system
4.3.4. provides certified base pipeline inventories and strapping charts for each pipeline/fuel tank on installation and coordinates with FMT to meet requirements IAW DLA E P-1

4.3.5. Paints, marks and color codes permanentely installed fuel facilities to comply with MIL-STD- 101B and Afman 91-203
4.3.6. Provides a list of emergency generators for Fuels facilities IAW AFI 32-1063
4.3.9. Contracts or provides for vegetation control and grass cutting in Fuels Management areas, including dikes and cut and cover fuel storage tanks

4.3.12. Coordinates with FMT to establish the following for fuels facilities;
Winterization program
Water accumulation program to prevent water intrusion
Indoor PMT Facility
Covered roof for LOX and LIN storage
Security fencing

4.3.13. Provides resources to inspect, clean, or deactivate tanks and remove tank bottoms and sludge from fuel tanks as required
4.3.13.1. Fuels personnel are not to assist in removing manhole covers or engage in any tank associated with inspection or cleaning
4.4.1. Coordinates refueling, ground products, and cryo support requirements with FSC by providing sortie data, accurate qty estimates, and proper fuel grade requests

4.4.1.1. Notifies FSC if any contamination is suspected and provides verification of last fuel grade issued to aircraft for defuel
4.4.1.2. Provides flying schedules and promptly notifies FSC of any changes
4.4.2. Assists in filling cryo servicing carts by performing safety person responsibilities outside of established servicing hours
4.4.3. Coordinates w/ Airfield Mgmt and FMT to establish aircraft fuel servicing priorities when not outlined in Base Support Plan (BSP) or Expeditionary Site Plan (ESP)

4.4.4. Institutes program to recover and reclaim petroleum products IAWAFI 23-502 and TO 42B-1-23
4.4.4.1. Coordinates with FMF to reclaim petroleum product

4.5.1. Conducts required surveys and inspections. Performs safety inspections on the FMF once a year or when requested by FMT.
4.5.1.1. Reviews and approves the selection and use of workplace specific PPE
4.5.2. Completes evaluations when workplace ops change and/or when new haz materials are introduced, processed or procedures are changed or engineering controls are modified or added IAW Afman 91-203

5.1.1. Comprised of a Fuels Mgmt Flight Commander and a Fuels Manager or Superintendent. At locations that do not earn a FMFC, may reference Fuels Mgmt Flight Leadership. At contracted locations, will be met by contract service provider.
5.1.1.1. Reserved for those in the rank of CMSgt and posses the Primary AF Specialty Code (PAFSC) 2F000
5.1.1.2 Exclusive to those in the rank of SMSgt or MSgt who posses the PAFSC 2F071, attended PLMC or posses 2F091

5.1.2.1. Provides optimal mission support, foster innovation, and provide quality of life initiatives geared toward building a robust and resilient FMF
5.1.2.2. Compiles with AF inspection program IAW AFI 90-201 AF Inspection System

5.1.2.3. Reviews eligible augmentees and nominates those most qualified to support the AF inspection system
5.1.2.4. Reviews and manages self-assessment checklist for currency and applicability.

5.1.2.6. Ensure cross flow of info using POL Monthly Incident Slides and weekly issues report, promotes Back-to-Basics campaign, and incorporates Higher Headquarters correspondence and disseminates fuels community information.
5.1.2.7. Follows requirements, procedures and guidelines outlined in FTLs, TCTOs, Service Bulletins, Incident Alerts, and other prescribing authoritative directives as required
5.1.2.8.1. Initiates discovery and supports collection efforts regarding inefficiencies, shortcomings, innovations, and best practices relating to the Fuels Mgmt career field.

5.1.2.8.2. Trip Reports. Documents support, feasibility and feedback during contingency and humanitarian ops, deployments, and major theater exercises.
5.1.2.8.3. After Action Reports, Captures comments and recommendations in order to assist and benefit future fuel conferences and working groups
5.1.2.9. Provides bulk petroleum forecasts, receipts, storage, issue, quality, and the accounting of cryo, gases, additives and hypergolic products (submits to AFSPC)
5.1.2.11. Develops base support plans (BSP) or expeditionary support plans (ESP) for the following: Operation, Contingency and Exercises

Establishes and FMF Organization email box and forwards to AFPA for incorporation into the AF Fuels Directory to facilitate info flow, ease dissemination of messages and provides orgs an efficient means of correspondence.

5.1.2.13. Establishes vehicle, equip, facility, and personnel minimum essential levels (MELs)
5.1.2.13.1. coordinates fixed systems filter element requirements with BCE
5.1.2.13.1.1. Funding and reimbursement procedures are outlined in DLA E 1-18.

5.1.2.13.2. Establishes vehicle filter element special inventory levels with VM
5.1.2.14. Coordinates with BCE, AFPA and MAJCOM Fuels on scheduled maintenance and tank cleaning.

5.2.2. Immediately notifies AFPA Current Ops (within 2 hours of incident) and submits a follow up message within 24 hours of occurrence with an info copy to the MAJCOM.
5.2.3. Ensures AFPA and DLA regional quality assurance representatives (QARs) are contacted to facilitate disposition instructions.
5.3.3. IAW AFI 36-2201; Completes AF Training Course, Possess a 7-skill level or higher, maintains task quals, proficiencies, and SEI for which individuals are certified, appointed by LRS/CC
5.3.3. Requests and submits Aeronautical Orders to the local HARM office for ABFDS crews once tasked to support UTC JFABF. Further guidance in AFI 11-402

5.3.5. Ensures program comlies with AFI 11-235, Appoints 2F071 as FARP team chief with 035 SEI. Additional duties will not be assigned to team chief. Trains and qualifies personnel to meet mission readiness. Secure storage area for personnel and equipment. Provides monthly FARP program status report to MAJCOM due the first Thursday of the month, certified by FMT

5.3.6. Holds 5-skill level or higher as a qualified fuels specialist. Holds rank of MSgt and below. Be medically qualified according to AFI 44-170 and AFI 48-123 and documented of AF Form 702, classified with code of 9c

5.3.6.5. Completes FARP initial qual course Phase I, II, and III training as well as recurring training with 11-235, posses 6 month experience (time starts when assigned to team), Perform min. of 4 FARP missions and hook ups.

qas.q: Reoccuring and JIT training for ABFDS/FARP operators
qas.a:
5.3.7. Physiological training, initial training conducted via altitude chamber, refresher training every 5 years with ROBD or altitude chamber
Weapons training
Life Support Equip training
Local Aircraft Ground Egress training
Local Night Vision Device training
SERE initial and refresher training
(ECAC FOR ABFDS)
